title: EinsteinEquation
summary: Packages the Einstein equation and parity/expansion certificates used by later PE constructions.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Packages the Einstein equation and parity/expansion certificates used by later PE constructions.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/EinsteinEquation.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: construct these certificates from ambient Ricci-flatness. - divided by.
correspondence.leanType: theorem can construct these certificates from ambient Ricci-flatness. -/

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal boundary metric coefficients in the expansion of h_r. Its mathematical data are: coeff: Nat maps to Metric Symbol X.boundary; even to order: for every m : Nat, m < X.boundary Dim maps to Odd m maps to coeff m equals 0; tracefree obstruction: Prop.
The parity predicate used by PE normal-form recursion. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, its defining expression is for every m : Nat, m < N maps to Odd m maps to E.coeff m equals 0.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{HasEvenExpansionToOrder}(N)=\forall m \colon \mathbb{N}, m < N \longrightarrow \operatorname{Odd} m \longrightarrow E.\operatorname{coeff} m = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: E.Has Even Expansion To Order X.boundary Dim.

Certificate that a Poincare normal form solves the Einstein equation. This is data-valued because it carries the formal expansion produced by the Einstein recursion, not merely a proposition about an externally fixed series. Its mathematical data are: einstein equation: NF.metric.ricci equals einstein RHS X NF.metric.g Plus; formal Expansion: PEFormal Expansion X; coeff zero: formal Expansion.coeff 0 equals NF.h0; expansion certificate: Prop.
correspondence.leanType: structure IsPoincareEinstein {X : PESpace.{u, v}} (NF : PoincareNormalFormData.{u, v, w} X)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For X of type PESpace, NF of type Poincare Normal Form Data X, and hPE of type Is Poincare Einstein NF, h PE.formal Expansion.Has Even Expansion To Order X.boundary Dim.
correspondence.leanType: theorem PE_expansion_even_to_order {X : PESpace} {NF : PoincareNormalFormData X} (hPE : IsPoincareEinstein NF) : hPE.formalExpansion.HasEvenExpansionToOrder X.boundaryDim

references.note: Source for Poincaré normal form and its relation to straight ambient metrics.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
